summary: boardstep preinstall script downloads and executes remote Windows binary
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "node install.js"`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. install.js is obfuscated using the obfuscator.io string-array transform (rotating array `_0x5cff` with decoder `_0x2352`; all literals wrapped as `_0x...` hex-named variables) and reconstructs the URL `https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe` by concatenating string-array fragments (`'https://ww' + 'w.pooron.o' + 'rg/tester.' + 'exe'`). The script downloads that executable to the OS temp directory as `tester_<randomhex>.exe`, chmods it 755, and detached-spawns it (directly on Windows and Linux, via `open` on macOS). The fetched binary is not pinned by version or hash, is hosted on a domain unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a kanban-board utility has no need for a remote Windows.exe), and the obfuscation is solely there to hide this behavior from scanners and reviewers. Any developer or CI agent that runs `npm install boardstep` automatically executes attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
